title: Floor and Ceil in a BST

content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Floor and Ceil in a BST". Refer to LeetCode description: N/A
constraints: 1 <= Number of nodes in the BST <= 10^5
1 <= Value of each node in the BST <= 10^9
content.inputFormat: The input will contain a binary search tree (BST) and a target value. The BST will be represented as a list of nodes where each node is a list containing the node's value and references to its left and right children. The target value will be an integer.
content.outputFormat: The output will be a list containing two values: the floor and the ceiling of the target value in the BST. If the target value is less than the smallest value in the BST, the floor will be null. If the target value is greater than the largest value in the BST, the ceiling will be null.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed
general mixed case
hidden.input: [[8, [3, [1, null, null], [6, [4, null, null], [7, null, null]]], [10, [14, [13, null, null], null], null]], 4.428571]

solution.approach: Recursive or Iterative traversal of the BST to find the floor and ceiling of the target value
solution.timeComplexity: O(h) where h is the height of the BST
solution.spaceComplexity: O(h) where h is the height of the BST

code.Python3:
class Node:
    def __init__(self, key):
        self.val = key
        self.left = None
        self.right = None

# Function to find the floor and ceiling of a target value in a BST
def find_floor_ceiling(root, target):
    floor = None
    ceiling = None

    while root:
        if root.val == target:
            floor = ceiling = root.val
            break
        elif root.val < target:
            floor = root.val
            root = root.right
        else:
            ceiling = root.val
            root = root.left

    return floor, ceiling

# Create a sample BST
root = Node(8)
root.left = Node(3)
root.right = Node(10)
root.left.left = Node(1)
root.left.right = Node(6)
root.left.right.left = Node(4)
root.left.right.right = Node(7)
root.right.right = Node(14)
root.right.right.left = Node(13)

target = 4
floor, ceiling = find_floor_ceiling(root, target)
print(f"Floor: {floor}, Ceiling: {ceiling}")
